seoTitle: Link Cell Data Between Sheets in Smartsheet
metaDescription: Set up live cell links between Smartsheet sheets so master portfolios and summaries update automatically without manual copy-pasting.
intro: Copy-pasting data between sheets breaks the moment the source changes. Smartsheet's cell linking feature keeps a live connection instead, so your summary or portfolio sheet updates automatically. This guide covers setting up that link and reading the visual indicators once it's active.

steps.heading: Understand live links vs. copy-paste
steps.body: A live cell link keeps data synced with its source, unlike a static copy-paste that never updates again.

steps.heading: Start the link from your destination cell
steps.body: Select the target cell and use the Link from Cell in Other Sheet feature to begin the connection.

steps.heading: Select your source sheet
steps.body: Navigate your workspace directory inside the link wizard to locate and choose the sheet you want to pull data from.

steps.heading: Bulk-link a range of cells
steps.body: Select multi-row data blocks or full status ranges to pull rolled-up metrics without breaking your layout.

steps.heading: Read the link direction indicators
steps.body: Look for the blue arrow icons that show whether a cell is receiving an inbound feed or sending an outbound one.

faq.question: What's the difference between linking cells and copy-pasting?
faq.answer: A cell link stays live and updates automatically when the source changes, while copy-pasting only captures a static snapshot.

faq.question: Can I link a whole range of cells at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can bulk-select multi-row data blocks or status ranges to link them together in one step.

faq.question: How do I tell if a cell is linked?
faq.answer: Blue arrow icons indicate the link direction, showing whether the cell is receiving data or sending it elsewhere.

faq.question: Will restructuring my columns break the link?
faq.answer: It can, so it's best practice to preserve cell links carefully when renaming templates or archiving rows.
